The drug that you are talking about has been in the research by Dr. Niels Pedersen. who is a researcher and vet from UC Davis. So i would not doubt the origin of the drug. It has shown effect in clinical trial, which you can read from the following paper: https://drive.google.com/file/d/0B_QRGPH6lbHtYlJrLUJkbklsZGs/view.

But two things bear in mind: 1) it has not been approved by US Food and Drug Administration (https://news.vin.com/vinnews.aspx?articleId=54548), meaning that the success of clinical trial does not necessarily make it an approved drug yet.

Second, which is the most important, is that what you are buying from China ia black market drug. Even Dr. Niels Pedersen has addressed this issue personally, please read: https://www.zenbycat.org/blog/update-by-dr-niels-pedersen-on-the-use-of-black-market-drugs-to-cure-fip.

It is not my intention to ask you not to save your cat. My question is can the drug you are buying is the same as the one that has been tested in clinical trial? Since you are considering getting a loan to buy a black market drug, i really hope you can think about the possibility of palliative treatment.
